behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (three collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary input), not a single-function algorithm. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network calls are not required to catch this specific failure; in-process source fakes returning distinct fixtures can expose dropped/miswired fields. LLM-DEP: behavior does not assert model judgment, only payload completeness before generation. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered because behavior says 'retrieved from all three sources', but the boundary token 'content passed to the summary-generation step' disqualifies needing real HTTP/OS for minimum failure detection.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real aggregation/report modules together with three deterministic source adapters (or stub clients) that each emit unique sentinel data. Execute collection+assembly and assert the exact summary-input payload contains all three source sections/keys and provenance, with no source silently omitted.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in cross-module error propagation (source failure -> section-level fallback note -> report continues). Unit is too low because it spans collector, assembler, and renderer behavior. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: unavailability can be represented by injected timeout/error responses from one source client; real network/OS is not strictly required for this behavior wording. LLM-DEP: correctness is fallback handling, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: no chained agentic decisions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE side-check: (a) no required real OS/network primitive in wording, (b) no model-output dependence, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM-stage handoff requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two sources returning valid data and one source client returning an availability error. Assert process exits successfully, final report includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this behavior is about output-content fidelity ('accurately represents key values'), which depends on the generator’s actual language/model decisions; deterministic stubs can hide real misrepresentation failures. MOCK-HIDE: replacing generation with a fixed mock would always pass formatting checks while missing factual drift/hallucination. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic judgment boundary is required, so not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but disqualified by boundary tokens 'narrative summary' and 'accurately represents' that target generation quality rather than wrapper plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Use the production summary-generation dependency with a fixed input dataset containing unambiguous key values. Evaluate generated narrative against factual assertions (e.g., required numbers/entities/trends must match source values, with tolerance rules) across repeated runs to detect model-output fidelity failures.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to an actual inbox, crossing real HTTP/network/email transport boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail transport can pass while real SMTP/API auth, routing, or inbox acceptance fails. MOCK-MISS: spans multiple modules and external boundary, beyond Integration minimum. LLM-DEP: behavior does not evaluate model quality; any generation step is incidental to delivery assertion. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow/Agentic were considered, but disqualified by boundary token 'reaches ... inbox correctly' (transport outcome, not multiple model decisions or model-quality scoring).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled job (or equivalent run path) against real reachable dependencies in a test environment, including actual email service credentials and a test recipient mailbox. Assert the message is received in inbox with expected report structure/content envelope and no pipeline abort.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure mode includes external service reachability plus durable local filesystem persistence and subsequent scheduled-run retry behavior (time/process boundary). MOCK-HIDE: in-memory fakes can miss real file-write/path/permission/timing issues and true service-unreachable behavior. MOCK-MISS: this is cross-run stateful recovery across modules and infrastructure. LLM-DEP: not dependent on model judgment. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but disqualified by behavior tokens 'saved to a local file' and 'retried on the next scheduled run,' which require real OS persistence/scheduling boundary for minimum realistic failure capture.
behaviors.test_description: Force real email endpoint unreachability in a test environment, run the job, and verify fallback file is persisted at configured path. Trigger the next scheduled execution with service restored and assert the persisted report is retried/sent, then confirm receipt and recovery state cleanup/retention policy.
